channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How to watch Wigan vs Hull KR: live stream 2025 Super League Grand Final rugby
item.description: How to watch 2025 Super League Grand Final live streams online, with the Wigan vs Hull KR clash carrying the potential for a seismic changing of the guard.
item.dc:creator: Aatif Sulleyman, TechRadar

item.title: Peacemaker season 3: everything we know about the DC Universe TV show's possible return
item.description: Peacemaker season 3 isn't on the cards, but this isn't the end of the line for its eccentric cast of characters.
item.dc:creator: Tom Power, TechRadar

item.title: Crypto spent millions to defeat Sherrod Brown and elect allies. It's ready for a repeat in 2026
item.description: The cryptocurrency industry spent millions last year to unseat a longtime critic, Democratic Sen. Sherrod Brown in Ohio, and install its allies in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Joey Cappelletti, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Apple Mac roadmap for 2025 and 2026 may have leaked – through macOS Tahoe 26
item.description: We could get new MacBook Pros, MacBook Airs, Mac minis, Mac Studios, and iMacs across the next few months.
item.dc:creator: David Nield, TechRadar

item.title: ICYMI: the week's 7 biggest tech stories, from Xbox Game Pass controversy to our Wake Up Dead Man review
item.description: The biggest tech news stories from Microsoft, Tag Heuer, Google, Netflix and more for October 11, 2025.
item.dc:creator: Hamish Hector, TechRadar

item.title: Apple doubles security bounty at Hexagon 2025
item.description: If you know your way around platform security, you don’t need to sell your discoveries to dodgy surveillance-as-a-service firms — Apple will make you a millionaire.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: CIOs turn to AI to assist with IT purchasing decisions
item.description: IT leaders are increasingly relying on AI to help with their purchasing decisions, with more than eight in 10 IT decision-makers saying they rely on the technology to make sense of the vendor and product landscape.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to watch South Korea vs Brazil for *FREE*
item.description: Here's our quick guide on how to watch South Korea vs Brazil with both teams already qualified for the 2026 World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Jones, TechRadar

item.title: Tesla’s ‘full self-driving’ faces another federal safety probe
item.description: US auto safety regulators are investigating Tesla Inc. over incidents in which its vehicles drove through red lights and violated other traffic laws while using the company's partial-automation software.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Larry Magid: For AI and Zoom, Pixel 10 Pro leaves iPhone behind
item.description: With its voice-controlled editing magic, AI powered 100x zoom and Gemini’s deep OS integration the Pixel 10 Pro pushes boundaries in ways that are both innovative and useful.
item.dc:creator: Larry Magid, The Mercury News

item.title: Princess of Wales suggests parents should put down the phone to combat social discontent
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The Princess of Wales has a suggestion for parents: Please put down the phone. Kate, as she is commonly known, collaborated with adult development researcher Robert Waldinger to warn that technology is contributing to an epidemic of disconnection that is hurting family relationships.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Ping Identity’s Kelvin Brewer on ensuring flexible identity service
item.description: Risk-based decisions should drive ICAM capabilities whether on premise, in the cloud or in hybrid environments, says Ping Identity engineering leader. The post Cyber Leaders Exchange 2025: Ping Identity’s Kelvin Brewer on ensuring flexible identity service first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Gemini CLI Extensions bring third-party tools into Google’s AI command line
item.description: Google is expanding the reach of its developer-focused AI tools with the launch of Gemini CLI Extensions, a new system that lets third-party developers integrate their own services directly into the Gemini command-line interface.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: React JS library moving from Meta to the Linux Foundation
item.description: React, a popular open source JavaScript library for web and native user interfaces, will be transferred from Meta to the React Foundation, a new organization being formed under the Linux Foundation. The new foundation will be the home for React, React Native, and supporting projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: From pilot to production: How large enterprises can scale XR projects
item.description: Broad adoption of augmented and virtual reality (AR and VR) technologies remains at an early stage, but many businesses are already seeing value in the systems they’ve rolled out: immersive training for frontline employees, for example, or engineers who can collaborate on design using 3D models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: With Arduino deal, Qualcomm pushes deeper into open-source and edge AI development
item.description: Qualcomm will acquire Arduino, the open-source hardware and software platform popular with millions of developers, in a move aimed at expanding its reach into the robotics, IoT, and AI edge ecosystems.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Key crypto agency on uncertain footing after White House pulls nominee
item.description: The White House’s decision to pull its nominee to lead the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has left the financial regulator in an increasingly uncertain position, with just one remaining commissioner in place to tackle President Trump’s ambitious crypto agenda.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: What is JPA? Introduction to Java persistence
item.description: The Jakarta Persistence API (JPA) is a specification that defines a standardized framework for data persistence. Persistence is the fundamental mechanism for saving and retrieving data over time. In Java, the data we persist is held in objects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: How GitHub won software development
item.description: In the late 2000s, I was working at Borland, the formerly high-flying software development tools company. My boss and I would regularly sit around tossing out ideas for new products. The Internet was really hitting its stride, and we saw numerous opportunities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nobel Prize in chemistry goes to discovery that could trap C02 and bring water to deserts
item.description: Three scientists have won the Nobel Prize in chemistry for their development of new molecular structures that can trap vast quantities of gas inside.
item.dc:creator: Kostya Manenkov And Stefanie Dazio, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tesla offers cheaper versions of 2 electric vehicles in bid to win back market share in tough year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Tesla rolled out new, cheaper versions of two of its electric car models on Tuesday in hopes the offerings will help revive flagging sales but investors dumped its stock anyway.
item.dc:creator: BERNARD CONDON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Google's Play Store shake-up looms after Supreme Court refuses to delay overhaul of the monopoly
item.description: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday refused to protect Google from a year-old order requiring a major makeover of its Android app store that's designed to unleash more competition against a system that a jury declared an illegal monopoly.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: OpenAI and chipmaker AMD sign chip supply partnership for AI infrastructure
item.description: Semiconductor maker AMD will supply its chips to artificial intelligence company OpenAI as part of an agreement to team up on building AI infrastructure, the companies said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5
